claim: Cross-check confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch already supports WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence while requiring project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt explicitly instructs: Every evidence record must include scope (project/external/user/unknown); WebFetch/WebSearch records must be marked as scope=external and cannot be used as project facts.
evidence.why: Evidence dispatch prompt contract enforces scope separation at the agent instruction level.

evidence.summary: Schema definition states evidence is an array of records with scope field, and explicitly notes that project facts require project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Evidence result schema contract validates scope requirements.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() implements two blocking codes: PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED when project scope lacks path locator, and PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when evidence is external-only.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement blocks external-only evidence from satisfying project fact requirements.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states external evidence from WebFetch/WebSearch can only serve as background research, not proof of target-project implementation, and existing-project facts without project-scoped evidence must be blocked.
evidence.why: Product doctrine codifies scope separation requirement.

evidence.summary: Test case external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts validates that readiness is blocked with PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE error when all evidence is external scope.
evidence.why: Test coverage proves external evidence cannot stand alone for project facts.

evidence.summary: Test case agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization validates that evidence records missing scope field are blocked during result normalization with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error.
evidence.why: Test coverage proves scope declaration is enforced at runtime.

risks: If there are additional provider execution paths beyond the Claude provider that do not enforce the scope separation contract, external research could still be mistaken for project facts.

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all explorer claims. The evidence dispatch API and schema correctly support WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence (scope=external) while requiring concrete project-scoped evidence (scope=project with path) for target-project facts. The router blocks external-only evidence via two error codes: PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED and PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE. Test coverage validates both scope enforcement and external-only blocking.
